```sparql
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
SELECT ?winner WHERE {
  ?winner wdt:P166 wd:Q1453643
}
```

messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.

content.message:
To determine who won the first Grammy Award for Best New Artist, I need to:

1. Identify the property related to Grammy Awards and specifically the "Best New Artist" category.
2. Find the winner of the first such award (i.e., the earliest year).
3. Retrieve the name of that winner.

### Step 1: Search for properties related to Grammy Awards or "Best New Artist"
I will search for properties related to awards or "Best New Artist" in Wikidata to locate the relevant property.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. exhibited creator (wd:P10661 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "featured artist"), also known as "artist exhibited", "artist featured", "artist presented", "artist whose work is exhibited", "creator featured", etc.:
  - intellectual or creative entity (person or organization) responsible for the elements exhibited
2. NRJ artist ID (wd:P5292 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Nouvelle Radio Jeune artist ID"), also known as "Nouvelle Radio Jeune artist ID":
  - identifier for an artist on the French NRJ website
3. Find NZ Artists ID (wd:P6792 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "findnzartists"), also known as "Find New Zealand Artists ID", "findnzartists", "FNZA ID", "NZ Artists ID":
  - identifier for artists listed in Find New Zealand Artists online database
4. after a work by (wd:P1877 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "inspiring artist"), also known as "after work by", "artist who inspired this", "based on a work by", "copy of a work by", "inspirational artist", etc.:
  - artist whose work strongly inspired / was copied in this item
5. movement (wd:P135 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "music scene"), also known as "artistic movement", "artistic school", "art movement", "art style", "literary movement", etc.:
  - literary, artistic, scientific or philosophical movement or ... with this person or work. For political ideologies use P1142.
6. AnyDecentMusic album ID (wd:P8435 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "AnyDecentMusic ID"), also known as "ADM album ID", "ADM ID", "Any Decent Music? ID", "AnyDecentMusic ID":
  - identifier for album reviews at AnyDecentMusic
7. Awards & Winners artist ID (wd:P7184 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "Awards and Winners artist ID":
  - identifier for an artist on Awards & Winners site
8. pseudonym (wd:P742 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "artist name"), also known as "alias", "artistic name", "artist name", "code name", "heteronym", etc.:
  - alias used by someone (for nickname use P1449)
9. Album of the Year artist ID (wd:P7050 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "AOTY artist ID":
  - identifier for an artist on the Album of the Year (AOTY) website
10. debut participant (wd:P2318 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "debut country"), also known as "debut country", "first appearance for", "first-time participant":
  - participant for whom this is their debut appearance in a series of events

1. nominee (wd:P2453 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award nominee"), also known as "award nominee":
  - qualifier used with “nominated for” to specify which person or organization was nominated
2. award received (wd:P166 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "award won"), also known as "award", "awarded", "awards", "awards received", "award won", etc.:
  - award or recognition received by a person, organization or creative work
3. winner (wd:P1346 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "prizewinner"), also known as "1st place medalist", "awardee", "champ", "champion", "has winner", etc.:
  - winner of a competition or similar event, not to be used from ... qualified with "for work" (P1686)) nor for wars or battles
4. trophy awarded (wd:P4622 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "cup awarded"), also known as "cup awarded", "diploma awarded", "medal awarded":
  - trophy awarded at the end of a selection process or of a ... or finishers under the form of a cup, a medal, a diploma
5. grants (wd:P5460 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "awards"), also known as "awards", "bestows", "confers", "gives out":
  - confers degree, honor, award, prize, title, certificate or medal denoting achievement to a person or organization
6. nominated for (wd:P1411 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "nominated for award"), also known as "finalist for", "finalist for award", "have nomination to", "nominated for award", "nomination received", etc.:
  - award nomination received by a person, organisation or creative work (inspired from "award received" (Property:P166))
7. number of hits (wd:P9184 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "hits"), also known as "career hits", "career hits total", "career total hits", "H", "hits", etc.:
  - number of hits earned in a baseball career or game(s)
8. competition won (wd:P2522 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "winner of"), also known as "contest won", "event won", "winner of", "won", "won event":

9. genre (wd:P136 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "genres"), also known as "artistic genre", "art style", "atlas genre", "film genre", "genre of literature", etc.:

10. tracklist (wd:P658 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "songs"), also known as "list of tracks", "songs", "track list", "track listing", "tracks":
  - audio tracks contained in this release
